follow_up: Investigate skill audit/thread association or normalize skill expectations for market sentiment scenarios.
rationale: Live service run completed through Python Agent + LiteLLM + Postgres, but trace gate failed because expected skill market-research was not audited for q001.

Live OpenRouter scenario q001 passed trace gates after prompt/skill retrieval fix: skill_found + skill_used are thread-scoped and market-research was observed; memory_recall and llm_response also present.
Second live OpenRouter scenario q001 passed trace gates and completion scoring after SSE-finish fallback: completed=true, fitness_score=1.0, required market-research skill observed.

follow_up: Free SSD space or move Podman storage, pull/build sandbox code image, restart agent with OPENSANDBOX_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8080 and rerun live_probe.
rationale: Full live probe completed through real service with chart, portfolio and memory gates passing, but sandbox_execute gate failed because OpenSandbox code image/runtime is unavailable on this machine after SDK import and consent fixes.
Kept: no-tool runner-parity passes with no tools, no skills, and 215 total tokens after tool-disable propagation plus skill stopword/query gating.
Kept: memory lifecycle scenarios pass after dedicated memory tool timeout budget; observed Fusion route with verbatim and summary_async providers.
Meta-Harness live probes for chart and portfolio pass through dispatcher with tool calls, SSE finish, clean output, retained model pinning, and bounded post-answer memory retain.

follow_up: Pareto candidate: reduce memory prompt skill over-selection and warm/accelerate post-answer retain.
rationale: Explicit memory live probe passes with memory_add and memory_search over Fusion, including verbatim and summary_async providers. Remaining issue is skill over-selection and retain latency, not memory tool correctness.

follow_up: Start OpenSandbox/dev stack or add an explicit sandbox-unavailable fixture gate before treating sandbox probe as agent regression.
rationale: Agent selected sandbox_execute and consent path worked, but OpenSandbox endpoint was unreachable, causing ConnectError and tool success 0.0. Defer until sandbox service is started or mocked for live probes.

follow_up: Add deterministic guard or trace-gate warning for duplicate memory_add calls with the same normalized content in one turn.
rationale: Trace gates and completion passed for two memory lifecycle scenarios, but the candidate is Pareto-dominated by existing runs and exposed duplicate memory_add calls plus high token/latency cost on the explicit add/search scenario. Use as evidence for a memory-tool dedup/latency hardening follow-up, not promotion.

follow_up: Add a trace-gate warning that explicitly counts duplicate memory_add normalized-content writes so future regressions are visible in scores.
rationale: Code candidate kept: it fixes a concrete duplicate memory_add write pattern observed in run-eeb4e11fab0f. The rerun passed both memory lifecycle trace gates, kept completion at 1.0, improved fitness from 0.8476 to 0.8583, and reduced total tokens from 60506 to 36959 and avg duration from 74463ms to 22902ms. It remains globally Pareto-dominated by unrelated tiny/no-tool candidates, so promotion is based on scenario-specific regression value.
Regression passed after adding global KG boundary trace gates; memory lifecycle still passes with Fusion route/provider metadata. Skill over-selection remains tracked by T098.

Kept: explicit memory and compaction scenarios now load only memory-usage while memory lifecycle gates remain green. This closes T098 for memory-only prompts; domain prompts can still load trading/risk/research when they carry domain intent.

Discarded/superseded: zero-overlap BM25 filtering passed memory gates but did not reduce memory-prompt skill over-selection in live traces; run-9b2b8bf9a58a supersedes it with memory-intent-only selection.
Stricter deterministic memory correctness gates passed on memory lifecycle scenarios: exact recall term, forbidden stale term, evidence term and memory metadata key checks.
Only retrieval candidate passing both trading/geo multi-hop and plain document QA canaries; keep fused vector+KG as current baseline while live adapters mature.
Vector-only passes plain document QA but fails trading/geo multi-hop boundary because KG source and claim reference are missing.
KG-only retrieves the KG claim but fails vector evidence requirements and overuses graph evidence for plain document QA.

follow_up: Evaluate docling/marker/mineru or post-processing candidates for formula, figure and code-fence preservation.
rationale: Real ResearchWatcher PDF-to-Markdown ground-truth benchmark passes with high text recall and phrase coverage; keep as fast baseline while deferring structured formula/figure/code-fence improvements to parser candidates.

follow_up: Configure a funded OpenRouter key or another real LiteLLM provider, then rerun lp-chart-001 and memory/tool scenarios.
rationale: Real agent/LiteLLM path reached OpenRouter, but provider returned 402 insufficient credits before tool use; keep runner/credential fix, block live-provider promotion until funded or alternate real provider is configured.

follow_up: Run remaining live_probe scenarios under free-router budget with pacing/backoff; switch to paid/verified provider for larger loops.
rationale: OpenRouter Free Models Router path passed live agent Meta-Harness probe: LiteLLM model openrouter/openrouter/free completed lp-chart-001 with get_chart_state tool_call/tool_result and trace gate pass. Prefer free router for free-tier dev runs; avoid openrouter/auto unless paid credits are available.
